name: worktree-lifecycle
title: Worktree Lifecycle — Branch-Aware Registry, Setup Readiness, Git-First Reconciliation
purpose: Capability: Branch-aware worktree registry with strict setup readiness, git-first workspace reconciliation, resume/materialization tool, main-checkout safety guard, and cleanup eligibility tracking.

requirements.title: Branch-Aware Worktree Registry
requirements.body: The worktree registry (per-change workflow state, with cross-change visibility via AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths Temporal search attributes) must store per-entry: branch, path, materialized flag, changeId, status, setupReady, setupFailureReason, baseRef, headSha, source, cleanupEligible, cleanupBlockedBy. Legacy MaterializedWorktreeRecord consumers must continue to work through a materialized-only compatibility view.

scenarios.title: Branch-only record survives worktree delete
given: A worktree is deleted via git but the registry entry retains branch
then: The entry exists with materialized=false and path=''

scenarios.title: Materialized-only view omits branch-only records
given: Registry has both materialized and branch-only entries
scenarios.when: A legacy consumer requests materialized worktrees
then: Only entries with materialized=true are returned

requirements.title: Cross-change worktree reads isolate poisoned workflow queries
requirements.body: Cross-change worktree visibility MUST query each owning change workflow independently. A failed per-change getWorktreesQuery MUST NOT abort the whole listWorktreesAcrossChanges result. The result MUST include healthy records from other workflows plus structured warnings and poisonedWorkflows metadata when describe/error evidence identifies poisoned history. When the Temporal visibility source itself is unavailable, the result MUST be marked unavailable rather than throwing to the WIP aggregator.

scenarios.title: Per-change query failure returns partial results
given: Visibility lists two change workflows with active worktrees
The first getWorktreesQuery succeeds
The second getWorktreesQuery fails
then: The first workflow's materialized worktree records are returned
A warning is returned for the failed workflow
The function does not throw for the per-workflow query failure

scenarios.title: Poison evidence is structured
given: The failed workflow describe output contains TMPRL1100, NonDeterministic, or WorkflowTaskFailedCauseNonDeterministicError evidence
then: poisonedWorkflows includes changeId, workflowId, recoveryReason="poisoned_history", evidenceSummary, and message
No destructive recovery action is performed

scenarios.title: Visibility-source outage is explicit
given: Temporal service, workflow list, or getHandle is unavailable
then: The result has records: [] and unavailable: true
A worktree_visibility warning explains the source failure

requirements.title: Strict Setup Readiness
requirements.body: advWorktreeCreate must record materializing state before git worktree add, then transition to active/idle only after postCreate hooks succeed. Hook failure writes setup_failed status with setupReady=false and the failure reason. Active session registration must be blocked on setup_failed records.

scenarios.title: Hook failure produces setup_failed
given: advWorktreeCreate is called and postCreate hook times out or returns non-zero
scenarios.when: The create flow handles the failure
then: Registry entry has status=setup_failed
setupReady=false
setupFailureReason contains the error
Active session is NOT registered for this worktree

scenarios.title: Successful setup transitions to active
given: advWorktreeCreate is called and postCreate hooks succeed
then: Registry entry has status=active or idle
setupReady=true
Active session is registered

requirements.title: Git-First Workspace Reconciliation
requirements.body: A git-first scanner (scanGitWorkspaceFacts) must enumerate actual git worktrees and classify their relationship to registry entries. A pure reconciler (reconcileWorktreeRegistry) must produce classification actions without IO side effects, separating git IO from deterministic state transitions.

scenarios.title: Orphan git worktree detected
given: A git worktree exists on disk but has no registry entry
then: The reconciler produces an 'untracked' classification for that worktree

scenarios.title: Missing worktree detected
given: A registry entry claims materialized=true but no git worktree exists
then: The reconciler produces a 'missing' classification for that entry

requirements.title: Worktree Resume / Materialization Tool
requirements.body: advWorktreeResume must resolve a changeId or branch to an existing worktree record. It must block on setup_failed records (return SETUP_FAILED), reuse setup-ready materialized worktrees, and materialize branch-only records through the strict create path. It must return a concrete workdir path on success.

scenarios.title: Resume reuses ready worktree
given: A materialized worktree with setupReady=true exists for the change
scenarios.when: advWorktreeResume is called with the changeId
then: Returns the existing worktree path without creating a new one

scenarios.title: Resume blocks on setup_failed
given: A worktree record with status=setup_failed exists for the change
scenarios.when: advWorktreeResume is called with the changeId
then: Returns SETUP_FAILED result with branch, path, and reason

scenarios.title: Resume materializes branch-only record
given: A registry entry exists with materialized=false and a branch name
scenarios.when: advWorktreeResume is called with the changeId
then: Creates the git worktree through the strict create path
Returns the new worktree path

requirements.title: Cleanup Eligibility Tracking
requirements.body: Registry entries must track cleanupEligible and cleanupBlockedBy fields. Entries with status=merged and no active sessions should be cleanup-eligible. Entries with setup_failed or active sessions should not be cleanup-eligible, with cleanupBlockedBy documenting the reason.

scenarios.title: Merged entry is cleanup-eligible
given: A worktree entry has status=merged
No active sessions reference this worktree
then: cleanupEligible=true
cleanupBlockedBy is empty

scenarios.title: Active session blocks cleanup
given: A worktree entry has an active session
then: cleanupEligible=false
cleanupBlockedBy documents the active session

requirements.title: Terminal Worktree Cleanup Reaper
requirements.body: ADV must provide one shared terminal cleanup reaper for terminal ADV worktrees. The reaper must be reachable from archive, manual cleanup, status/triage discovery, bounded startup pending-delete drain, and best-effort session.deleted. Startup behavior must drain already-known pending deletes only; full terminal discovery must not run during plugin startup. All deletion attempts must delegate to advWorktreeDelete.

scenarios.title: Shared triggers reach the reaper
given: A terminal ADV worktree exists for an archived or closed change
scenarios.when: archive, manual cleanup, status/triage, startup, or session.deleted cleanup runs
then: The shared terminal cleanup reaper evaluates the candidate
The actual deletion attempt delegates to advWorktreeDelete

scenarios.title: Startup remains bounded
given: Plugin startup begins with pending deletes recorded
then: Only already-known pending deletes are drained
Full terminal discovery is not executed during startup

requirements.title: Terminal Cleanup Safety Gate
requirements.body: Terminal cleanup candidates MUST NOT run git worktree remove directly. advWorktreeDelete is the sole deletion authority and must verify durable ADV state, terminal owning change status (archived or closed), branch integration, clean worktree state, and no live process CWD before removal. census.cleanupEligible is advisory discovery/visibility data only and must not be used as sufficient deletion authority.

scenarios.title: Unsafe candidates are retained
given: A cleanup candidate is dirty, unmerged, non-terminal, or in use
scenarios.when: The terminal cleanup reaper evaluates it
then: The candidate is retained with a blocker
No direct git worktree remove command is run

scenarios.title: Census eligibility is not authority
given: census.cleanupEligible is true for a worktree
then: advWorktreeDelete still verifies durable ADV state before deletion

requirements.title: Terminal Cleanup Visibility
requirements.body: Retained terminal cleanup blockers must be visible without requiring manual git inspection. adv_status must surface aggregate retained-terminal-worktree counts/classes only. adv_worktree_triage must surface exact branches, paths, and blockers for retained terminal cleanup candidates.

scenarios.title: Status shows aggregates
given: Retained terminal cleanup blockers exist
then: The output includes retained cleanup counts/classes
The normal status surface does not dump every retained path

scenarios.title: Triage shows exact blockers
given: Retained terminal cleanup blockers exist
then: Exact branches, paths, and blockers are returned

requirements.title: Single Terminal Cleanup Lifecycle Path
requirements.body: Terminal cleanup processing must route through one shared cleanup path instead of duplicate lifecycle loops. Concurrent cleanup triggers must be serialized or idempotent, and retained in-use worktrees must stay queued or preserved for retry. Manual cleanup must be able to retry safe terminal pending deletes even when prior automatic attempts reached an attempt cap.

scenarios.title: Concurrent triggers are idempotent
given: Two cleanup triggers process the same pending delete
scenarios.when: Both triggers run concurrently or sequentially
then: Processing is serialized or idempotent
At most one deletion succeeds and retained state remains consistent

scenarios.title: Manual cleanup can retry exhausted safe items
given: A safe terminal pending delete has exhausted automatic attempts
then: The safe pending delete is retried through the shared path

requirements.title: Machine-Enforced ADV Worktree Mutation Guard
requirements.body: ADV mutating tools that advance gates or change task execution state must structurally block execution from the main checkout when feature_flags.worktree_guard_enforce is true (default per rq-autoManageAdvWorktrees AC2) OR when the per-change `worktree_auto_managed` marker is true (regardless of global flag). Mutations from an ADV worktree remain allowed. The proposal gate remains exempt so a change can be created before a worktree exists. For auto-managed changes, the guard MUST attempt to materialize the worktree via `advWorktreeResume` before BLOCKing, and surface the resulting path via `expectedWorktreePath` so the agent can re-run from the correct workdir. The guard must return WorktreeIsolationViolation with main checkout path and remediation instead of relying on agent-only instructions. Explicit `worktree_guard_enforce: false` preserves legacy permissive behavior for non-auto-managed changes.

scenarios.title: Gate completion blocks from main checkout
given: feature_flags.worktree_guard_enforce is true
The current session is in the main checkout
scenarios.when: adv_gate_complete is called for discovery, design, planning, execution, acceptance, or release
then: The tool returns WorktreeIsolationViolation before any state mutation
The response includes mainCheckoutPath and remediation
No gate completion signal is sent

scenarios.title: Task execution mutations block from main checkout
given: feature_flags.worktree_guard_enforce is true
The current session is in the main checkout
scenarios.when: adv_task_add or adv_task_update with in_progress, done, or cancelled status is called
then: The tool returns WorktreeIsolationViolation before any task signal mutation
The response includes mainCheckoutPath and remediation
Worktree-origin task mutations remain allowed

scenarios.title: Proposal gate and explicit-false paths remain compatible
given: The proposal gate is being completed or worktree_guard_enforce is explicitly false for a non-auto-managed change
scenarios.when: A supported ADV mutation executes from the main checkout
then: The proposal gate completion remains allowed so a worktree can be created after proposal
Explicit-false behavior preserves legacy mutation flow for non-auto-managed changes
The guard remains additive and does not alter read-only tools

requirements.title: Bounded Worktree Cleanup
requirements.body: Worktree cleanup operations must be bounded to prevent runaway deletion. After each worktree deletion, a post-delete notification must be emitted within the default 10s timeout. Per-item cleanup must proceed sequentially with bounded retries; batch or parallel unbounded cleanup is prohibited. The cleanup tool must classify worktrees into drift groups (safe, blocked, dirty/in-use, needs-investigation) and remain report-only for worktree drift even under `--execute`. Actual worktree deletion remains owned by `adv_worktree_delete` and `adv_worktree_cleanup`.

scenarios.title: Post-delete notification is bounded
given: A worktree deletion is requested via adv_worktree_delete or adv_worktree_cleanup
then: A post-delete notification is emitted within the default 10s timeout
No unbounded wait blocks subsequent operations

scenarios.title: Per-item sequential cleanup with bounded retries
given: Multiple worktrees are eligible for cleanup
then: Worktrees are processed sequentially, one at a time
Each item has a bounded retry count (max 5)
Batch or parallel unbounded deletion is not performed
